title: Flowise Execute Flow node SSRF: user-controlled base URL passed to axios without checkDenyList/secureFetch validation
description: In the run() method of ExecuteFlow_Agentflow in packages/components/nodes/agentflow/ExecuteFlow/ExecuteFlow.ts, the base URL used to call another flow is taken from the node's user-configurable executeFlowBaseURL input (baseURL = nodeData.inputs?.executeFlowBaseURL || options.baseURL). That value is concatenated into finalUrl (`${baseURL}/api/v1/prediction/${selectedFlowId}`) and issued directly with the raw axios client (await axios(requestConfig)) without ever calling the project's httpSecurity SSRF guards (checkDenyList / resolveAndValidate / secureAxiosRequest). An attacker who can configure the Execute Flow node (any authenticated user, or via an API with chatflow update permission) can therefore set the base URL to an internal/intranet address, cloud metadata endpoint (e.g. 169.254.169.254), or loopback service and trigger the request by hitting POST /api/v1/prediction/<chatflowId>, causing the server to perform a server-side request to the attacker-chosen internal address and echo back the response.
